idea.hook: Could a STAR fit inside a city?
facts: Our Sun: A GIGANTIC 1.39 MILLION km in diameter.
But not all stars are giants! Meet the Neutron Star: the ultra-dense core of a collapsed star.
A typical Neutron Star measures only about 20-25 km across.
idea.payoff: That means a star could be SMALLER than Manhattan (approx. 21.6 km long), fitting entirely within its borders!
idea.title: Star Smaller Than Your City?! 🤯🏙️🌌

scenes.text: Could a STAR fit inside a city?
scenes.visual: A stylized illustration of a vibrant cityscape at night, with a faint, shimmering star icon appearing above it, then a question mark animating in.
A majestic, detailed visual of the Sun, with swirling plasma. A small Earth-like planet orbits in the distance for subtle scale.

scenes.text: Not all stars are giants! Meet the Neutron Star.
scenes.visual: Transition from the large Sun to a dramatic animation of a collapsing star, then revealing a tiny, incredibly dense, rapidly spinning neutron star.
A close-up of the neutron star, its surface extremely smooth and dark, with a measuring tape graphic wrapping around it, highlighting the 20-25km scale.

scenes.text: A STAR smaller than Manhattan (21.6 km)!
scenes.visual: An animated, accurate satellite view of Manhattan Island. A tiny, glowing neutron star smoothly lands and settles within the island's borders, pulsing gently.
